verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The Father loveth the Son, and hath given all things into His hand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life, and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Great is shown to be in all things the gain of humility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus it is that we have brought arts to perfection, not by learning them all at once from our teachers; it is thus that we have built cities, putting them together slowly, little by little; it is thus that we maintain our life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And marvel not if the thing has so much power in matters pertaining to this life, when in spiritual things one may find that great is the power of this wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For so the Jews were enabled to be delivered from their idolatry, being led on gently and little by little, and hearing from the first nothing sublime concerning either doctrine or life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So after the coming of Christ, when it was the time for higher doctrines, the Apostles brought over all men without at first uttering anything sublime.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so Christ appears to have spoken to most at the beginning, and so John did now, speaking of Him as of some wonderful man, and darkly introducing high matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For instance, when commencing he spake thus: “A man cannot receive anything of himself” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 27 ): then after adding a high expression, and saying, “He that cometh from heaven is above all,” he again brings down his discourse to what is lowly, and besides many other things saith this, that “God giveth not the Spirit by measure.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then he proceeds to say, “The Father loveth the Son, and hath given all things into His hand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And after that, knowing that great is the force of punishment, and that the many are not so much led by the promise of good things as by the threat of the terrible, he concludes his discourse with these words; “He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life; but he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here again he refers the account of punishment to the Father, for he saith not “the wrath of the Son,” (yet He is the Judge,) but sets over them the Father, desiring so the more to terrify them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Is it then enough,” saith one, “to believe on the Son, that one may have eternal life?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And hear Christ Himself declaring this, and saying, “Not every one that saith unto Me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven” ( Matt. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 21 ); and the blasphemy against the Spirit is enough of itself to cast a man into hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why speak I of a portion of doctrine?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Though a man believe rightly on the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost, yet if he lead not a right life, his faith will avail nothing towards his salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore when He saith, “This is life eternal, that they may know Thee the only true God” ( c. xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ), let us not suppose that the (knowledge) spoken of is sufficient for our salvation; we need besides this a most exact life and conversation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since though he has said here, “He that believeth on the Son hath eternal life,” and in the same place something even stronger, (for he weaves his discourse not of blessings only, but of their contraries also, speaking thus: “He that believeth not the Son shall not see life, but the wrath of God abideth on him”;) yet not even from this do we assert that faith alone is sufficient to salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the directions for living given in many places of the Gospels show this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore he did not say, “This by itself is eternal life,” nor, “He that doth but believe on the Son hath eternal life,” but by both expressions he declared this, that the thing doth contain life, yet that if a right conversation follow not, there will follow a heavy punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And he did not say, “awaiteth him,” but, “abideth on him,” that is, “shall never remove from him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that thou mayest not think that the “shall not see life,” is a temporary death, but mayest believe that the punishment is continual, he hath put this expression to show that it rests upon him continually.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this he has done, by these very words forcing them on to Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore he gave not the admonition to them in particular, but made it universal, the manner which best might bring them over.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he did not say, “if ye believe,” and, “if ye believe not,” but made his speech general, so that his words might be free from suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this he has done yet more strongly than Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Christ saith, “He that believeth not is condemned already,” but John saith, “shall not see life, but the wrath of God abideth on him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With good cause; for it was a different thing for a man to speak of himself and for another to speak of him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They would have thought that Christ spake often of these things from self-love, and that he was a boaster; but John was clear from all suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if at a later time, Christ also used stronger expressions, it was when they had begun to conceive an exalted opinion of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When therefore Jesus knew how the Pharisees had heard that Jesus made and baptized more disciples than John, (though Jesus Himself baptized not but His disciples,) He left Judæa, and departed again into Galilee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He indeed baptized not, but they who carried the news, desiring to excite their hearers to envy, so reported.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Wherefore then ‘departed’ He?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not from fear, but to take away their malice, and to soften their envy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He was indeed able to restrain them when they came against Him, but this He would not do continually, that the Dispensation of the Flesh might not be disbelieved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He often been seized and escaped, this would have been suspected by many; therefore for the most part, He rather orders matters after the manner of a man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as He desired it to be believed that He was God, so also that, being God, He bore the flesh; therefore even after the Resurrection, He said to the disciple, “Handle Me and see, for a spirit hath not flesh and bones” ( Luke xxiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 39 ); therefore also He rebuked Peter when he said, “Be it far from Thee, this shall not be unto thee.” ( Matt. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So much was this matter an object of care to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is no small part of the doctrines of the Church; it is the chief point of the salvation wrought for us; by which all has been brought to pass, and has had success, for it was thus that the bonds of death were loosed, sin taken away, and the curse abolished, and ten thousand blessings introduced into our life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore He especially desired that the Dispensation should be believed, as having been the root and fountain of innumerable goods to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet while acting thus in regard of His Humanity, He did not allow His Divinity to be overcast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so, after His departure He again employed the same language as before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He went not away into Galilee simply, but in order to effect certain important matters, those among the Samaritans; nor did He dispense these matters simply, but with the wisdom that belonged to Him, and so as not to leave to the Jews any pretense even of a shameless excuse for themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to this the Evangelist points when he says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And He must needs go through Samaria.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Showing that He made this the by-work of the journey.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which also the Apostles did; for just as they, when persecuted by the Jews, came to the Gentiles; so also Christ, when the Jews drove Him out, then took the Samaritans in hand, as He did also in the case of the Syrophenician woman.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this was done that all defense might be cut away from the Jews, and that they might not be able to say, “He left us, and went to the uncircumcised.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore the disciples excusing themselves said, “It was necessary that the Word of God should first have been spoken unto you; but seeing ye judge yourselves unworthy of everlasting life, lo, we turn to the Gentiles.” ( Acts xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He saith again Himself, “I am not come but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel” ( Matt. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 24 ); and again, “It is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to give it to dogs.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when they drove Him away, they opened a door to the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not so did He come to the Gentiles expressly, but in passing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In passing then,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He cometh to a city of Samaria, which is called Sychar, near to the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son Joseph.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now Jacob’s well was there.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why is the Evangelist exact about the place?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is, that when thou hearest the woman say, “Jacob our father gave us this well,” thou mayest not think it strange.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this was the place where Levi and Simeon, being angry because of Dinah, wrought that cruel slaughter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And it may be worth while to relate from what sources the Samaritans were made up; since all this country is called Samaria.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence then did they receive their name?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The mountain was called “Somor” from its owner ( 1 Kings xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 24 ): as also Esaias saith, “and the head of Ephraim is Somoron” ( Isa. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 9 , LXX.), but the inhabitants were termed not “Samaritans” but “Israelites.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But as time went on, they offended God, and in the reign of Pekah, Tiglath-Pileser came up, and took many cities, and set upon Elah, and having slain him, gave the kingdom to Hoshea. ( 2 Kings xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Against him Shalmaneser came and took other cities, and made them subject and tributary. ( 2 Kings xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At first he yielded, but afterwards he revolted from the Assyrian rule, and betook himself to the alliance of the Ethiopians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Assyrian learnt this, and having made war upon them and destroyed their cities, he no longer allowed the nation to remain there, because he had such suspicions that they would revolt. ( 2 Kings xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he carried them to Babylon and to the Medes, and having brought thence nations from divers places, planted them in Samaria, that his dominion for the future might be sure, his own people occupying the place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After this, God, desiring to show that He had not given up the Jews through weakness, but because of the sins of those who were given up, sent lions against the foreigners, who ravaged all their nation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things were reported to the king, and he sent a priest to deliver to them the laws of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still not even so did they desist wholly from their impiety, but only by halves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But as time went on, they in turn abandoned their idols, and worshiped God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And when things were in this state, the Jews having returned, ever after entertained a jealous feeling towards them as strangers and enemies, and called them from the name of the mountain, “Samaritans.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From this cause also there was no little rivalry between them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Samaritans did not use all the Scriptures, but received the writings of Moses only, and made but little account of those of the Prophets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet they were eager to thrust themselves into the noble Jewish stock, and prided themselves upon Abraham, and called him their forefather, as being of Chaldæa; and Jacob also they called their father, as being his descendant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the Jews abominated them as well as all (other nations).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore they reproached Christ with this, saying, “Thou art a Samaritan, and hast a devil.” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And for this reason in the parable of the man that went down from Jerusalem to Jericho, Christ makes the man who showed pity upon him to have been “a Samaritan” ( Luke x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 33 ), one who by them was deemed mean, contemptible, and abominable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in the case of the ten lepers, He calls one a “stranger” on this account, (for “he was a Samaritan,”) and He gave His charge to the disciples in these words, “Go not into the way of the Gentiles, and into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not.” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor was it merely to describe the place that the Evangelist has reminded us of Jacob, but to show that the rejection of the Jews had happened long ago.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For during the time of their forefathers these Jews possessed the land, and not the Samaritans; and the very possessions which not being theirs, their forefathers had gotten, they being theirs, had lost by their sloth and transgressions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So little is the advantage of excellent ancestors, if their descendants be not like them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, the foreigners when they had only made trial of the lions, straightway returned to the right worship of the Jews, while they, after enduring such inflictions, were not even so brought to a sound mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To this place Christ now came, ever rejecting a sedentary and soft life, and exhibiting one laborious and active.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He useth no beast to carry Him, but walketh so much on a stretch, as even to be wearied with His journeying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this He ever teacheth, that a man should work for himself, go without superfluities, and not have many wants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nay, so desirous is He that we should be alienated from superfluities, that He abridgeth many even of necessary things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He said, “Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man hath not where to lay His head.” ( Matt. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He spent most of His time in the mountains, and in the deserts, not by day only, but also by night.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this David declared when he said, “He shall drink of the brook in the way” ( Ps. cx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 7 ): by this showing His frugal way of life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This too the Evangelist shows in this place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus therefore, being wearied with His journey, sat thus by the well; and it was about the sixth hour.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus saith unto her, Give Me to drink.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For His disciples were gone away into the city to buy meat.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hence we learn His activity in journeying, His carelessness about food, and how He treated it as a matter of minor importance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so the disciples were taught to use the like disposition themselves; for they took with them no provisions for the road.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this another Evangelist declares, saying, that when He spake to them concerning “the leaven of the Pharisees” ( Matt. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ), they thought that it was because they carried no bread; and when he introduces them plucking the ears of corn, and eating ( Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1 ), and when he saith that Jesus came to the fig-tree by reason of hunger ( Matt. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 18 ), it is for nothing else but only to instruct us by all these to despise the belly, and not to deem that its service is anxiously to be attended to.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe them, for instance, in this place neither bringing anything with them, nor because they brought not anything, caring for this at the very beginning and early part of the day, but buying food at the time when all other people were taking their meal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not like us, who the instant we rise from our beds attend to this before anything else, calling cooks and butlers, and giving our directions with all earnestness, applying ourselves afterwards to other matters, preferring temporal things to spiritual, valuing those things as necessary which we ought to have deemed of less importance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore all things are in confusion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We ought, on the contrary, making much account of all spiritual things, after having accomplished these, then to apply ourselves to the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in this place it is not His laboriousness alone that is shown, but also His freedom from pride; not merely by His being tired, nor by His sitting by the way-side, but by His having been left alone, and His disciples having been separated from Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet it was in His power, if He had willed it, either not to have sent them all away, or when they departed to have had other ministers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He would not; for so He accustomed His disciples to tread all pride beneath their feet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And what marvel,” saith one, “if they were moderate in their wishes, since they were fishermen and tentmakers?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Fishermen and tentmakers they were; but they had in a moment mounted even to the height of heaven, and had become more honorable than all earthly kings, being deemed worthy to become the companions of the Lord of the world, and to follow Him whom all beheld with awe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And ye know this too, that those men especially who are of humble origin, whenever they gain distinction, are the more easily lifted up to folly, because they are quite ignorant how to bear their sudden honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Restraining them therefore in their present humblemindedness, He taught them always to be moderate, and never to require any to wait upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He therefore,” saith the Evangelist, “being wearied with His journey, sat thus at the well.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that His sitting was because of weariness? because of the heat? because of his waiting for His disciples?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He knew, indeed, what should take place among the Samaritans, but it was not for this that He came principally; yet, though He came not for this, it behooved not to reject the woman who came to Him, when she manifested such a desire to learn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews, when He was even coming to them, drove Him away; they of the Gentiles, when He was proceeding in another direction, drew Him to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They envied, these believed on Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They were angry with, these revered and worshiped Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Was He to overlook the salvation of so many, to send away such noble zeal?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This would have been unworthy of His lovingkindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He ordered all the matter in hand with the Wisdom which became Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He sat resting His body and cooling It by the fountain; for it was the very middle of the day, as the Evangelist has declared, when he says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “It was about the sixth hour.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He sat “thus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What meaneth “thus”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not upon a throne, not upon a cushion, but simply, and as He was, upon the ground.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how he declareth that the woman came forth for another purpose, in every way silencing the shameless gainsaying of the Jews, that none might say that He acted in opposition to His own command, bidding (His disciples) not to enter into any city of the Samaritans, yet conversing with Samaritans. ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore the Evangelist has put,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For His disciples were gone away into the city to buy meat.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Bringing in many reasons for His conversation with her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What doth the woman?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When she heard, “Give Me to drink,” she very wisely makes the speech of Christ an occasion for a question, and saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How is it that thou, being a Jew, askest drink of me, which am a Samaritan?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Jews have no dealings with the Samaritans.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And whence did she suppose Him to be a Jew?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From His dress, perhaps, and from His dialect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe, I pray you, how considerate the woman was.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If there was need of caution, Jesus needed it, not she.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For she doth not say, “The Samaritans have no dealings with the Jews,” but, “The Jews do not admit the Samaritans.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet still, although free herself from blame, when she supposed that another was falling into it she would not even so hold her peace, but corrected, as she thought, what was done unlawfully.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps some one may ask how it was that Jesus asked drink of her, when the law did not permit it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If it be answered that it was because He knew beforehand that she would not give it, then for this very reason He ought not to have asked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then can we say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That the rejecting such observances as these was now a matter of indifference to Him; for He who induced others to do them away, would much more Himself pass them by.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Not that which goeth in,” saith He, “defileth a man, but that which goeth out.” ( Matt. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this conversation with the woman would be no slight charge against the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For often did He draw them to Himself, both by words and deeds, but they would not attend; while observe how she is detained by a simple request.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He did not as yet enter on the prosecution of this business, nor the way, yet if any came to Him He did not prevent them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to the disciples also He said thus, “Into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He did not say, “And when they come to you, reject them”; that would have been very unworthy of His lovingkindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore He answered the woman, and said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If thou knewest the gift of God and who it is that saith to thee, Give Me to drink, thou wouldest have asked of Him, and He would have given thee living water.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: First, He showeth that she is worthy to hear and not to be overlooked, and then He revealeth Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For she, as soon as she had learnt who He was, would straightway hearken and attend to Him; which none can say of the Jews, for they, when they had learned, asked nothing of Him, nor did they desire to be informed on any profitable matter, but insulted and drove Him away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when the woman had heard these words, observe how gently she answers:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep; from whence then hast thou that living water?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Already He hath raised her from her low opinion of Him, and from deeming that He is a common man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For not without a reason doth she here call Him, “Lord”; but assigning to Him high honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That she spake these words to honor Him, is plain from what is said afterwards, since she did not laugh nor mock, but doubted for a while.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And wonder not if she did not at once perceive all, for neither did Nicodemus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How can these things be?” and again, “How can a man be born when he is old?” and again, “Can he enter the second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this woman more reverently: “Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep; from whence then hast thou that living water?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ said one thing, and she imagined another, hearing nothing beyond the words, and as yet unable to form any lofty thought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet, had she spoken hastily, she might have said, “If thou hadst had that living water, thou wouldest not have asked of me, but wouldest rather have provided for thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou art but a boaster.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But she said nothing like this; she answers with much gentleness, both at first and afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For at first she saith, “How is it that thou, being a Jew, askest drink of me?” she saith not, as though speaking to an alien and an enemy, “Far be it from me to give to thee, who art a foe and a stranger to our nation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And afterwards again, when she heard Him utter great words, a thing at which enemies are most annoyed, she did not mock nor deride ; but what saith she?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Art thou greater than our father Jacob, which gave us the well, and drank thereof himself, and his children, and his cattle?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how she thrusts herself into the noble stock of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what she saith is somewhat of this kind: “Jacob used this water, and had nothing better to give us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this she said showing that from the first answer (of Christ) she had conceived a great and sublime thought; for by the words, “he drank thereof himself, and his children, and his cattle,” she implies nothing else, than that she had a notion of a better Water, but that she never found it, nor clearly knew it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: More clearly to explain what she means to say, the sense of her words is this: “Thou canst not assert that Jacob gave us this well, and used another himself; for he and his children drank of this one, which they would not have done if they had had another and a better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now of the water of this well it is not in thy power to give me, and thou canst not have another and a better, unless thou dost confess that thou art greater than Jacob.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence then hast thou that water which thou promisest that thou wilt give us?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews did not converse with Him thus mildly, and yet He spake to them on the same subject, making mention of the like water, but they profited nothing; and when He made mention of Abraham, they even attempted to stone Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not so does this woman approach Him; but with much gentleness, in the midst of the heat, at noon, she with much patience saith and hears all, and does not so much as think of what the Jews most probably would have asserted, that “This fellow is mad, and beside himself: he hath tied me to this fount and well, giving me nothing, but using big words”; no, she endures and perseveres until she has found what she seeks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If now a woman of Samaria is so earnest to learn something profitable, if she abides by Christ though not as yet knowing Him, what pardon shall we obtain, who both knowing Him, and being not by a well, nor in a desert place, nor at noon-day, nor beneath the scorching sunbeams, but at morning-tide, and beneath a roof like this, enjoying shade and comfort, yet cannot endure to hear anything that is said, but are wearied by it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not such was that woman; so occupied was she by Jesus’ words, that she even called others to hear them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews, on the contrary, not only did not call, but even hindered and impeded those who desired to come to Him, saying, “See, have any of the rulers believed on him? but this people, which knoweth not the Law, are cursed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then imitate this woman of Samaria; let us commune with Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even now He standeth in the midst of us, speaking to us by the Prophets and Disciples; let us hear and obey.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long shall we live uselessly and in vain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because, not to do what is well-pleasing to God is to live uselessly, or rather not merely uselessly, but to our own hurt; for when we have spent the time which has been given us on no good purpose, we shall depart this life to suffer severest punishment for our unseasonable extravagance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it can never be that a man who has received money to trade with, and then has eaten it up, shall have it required at his hands by the man who intrusted it to him; and that one who has spent such a life as ours to no purpose shall escape punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was not for this that God brought us into this present life, and breathed into us a soul, that we should make use of the present time only, but that we should do all our business with a regard to the life which is to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Things irrational only are useful for the present life; but we have an immortal soul, that we may use every means to prepare ourselves for that other life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if one enquire the use of horses and asses and oxen, and other such-like animals, we shall tell him that it is nothing else but only to minister to the present life; but this cannot be said of us; our best condition is that which follows on our departure hence; and we must do all that we may shine there, that we may join the choir of Angels, and stand before the King continually, through endless ages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore the soul is immortal, and the body shall be immortal too, that we may enjoy the never-ending blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if, when heavenly things are proffered thee, thou remainest nailed to earth, consider what an insult is offered to thy Benefactor, when He holdeth forth to thee things above, and thou, making no great account of them choosest earth instead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore, as despised by thee, He hath threatened thee with hell; that thou mayest learn hence of what great blessings thou deprivest thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: God grant that none make trial of that punishment, but that having been well-pleasing to Christ, we may obtain everlasting blessings, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ; to whom with the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, now and ever, and world without end.
